subject: Create filesystem watch script (watch-for-results.sh)
description:
Create a new shell script that uses filesystem events (`fswatch` on macOS, `inotifywait` on Linux) to detect result files immediately when agents complete, replacing the fixed-interval polling as the primary completion mechanism.

**What to implement:**

1. Create `claude/sdd-tools/scripts/watch-for-results.sh` (~60 lines):
   - Usage: `watch-for-results.sh <session_dir> <expected_count> [task_ids...]`
   - Check tool availability at startup: `command -v fswatch` / `command -v inotifywait`
   - If neither available: exit with code 2 (not-available signal)
   - Detect pre-existing result files before starting watch (count toward expected)
   - Watch for `Created` events only (not Modified) to avoid duplicates
   - On macOS: use `fswatch --event Created`
   - On Linux: use `inotifywait -m -e create`
   - Output one line per detection: `RESULT_FOUND: result-task-{id}.md ({found}/{expected})`
   - Output `ALL_DONE` when all expected result files found
   - Configurable timeout via `WATCH_TIMEOUT` env var (default: 2700 seconds = 45 min)
   - Exit codes: 0 (all found), 1 (timeout), 2 (tools unavailable)

**Files to create:**
- `claude/sdd-tools/scripts/watch-for-results.sh` (new, ~60 lines)

**Acceptance Criteria:**

_Functional:_
- [ ] Script detects result files within 1 second of creation (zero-latency)
- [ ] Emits `RESULT_FOUND: result-task-{id}.md (N/M)` for each detection
- [ ] Emits `ALL_DONE` when expected count reached
- [ ] Exits 0 when all results found
- [ ] Pre-existing result files counted before watch starts
- [ ] Accepts session_dir, expected_count, and optional task_ids as arguments

_Edge Cases:_
- [ ] Handles result files already present at watch start
- [ ] Ignores non-result files created in session directory
- [ ] Agent creates temp file then renames → watch triggers on final filename only
- [ ] Partial completion at timeout → reports found results, exits code 1

_Error Handling:_
- [ ] Neither fswatch nor inotifywait available → exit code 2
- [ ] fswatch exits unexpectedly → script exits code 1 (orchestrator falls back to polling)
- [ ] Timeout reached → exit code 1

_Performance:_
- [ ] Detection latency < 1 second from file creation

**Testing Requirements:**
• Shell (bats): All results found → ALL_DONE output, exit 0
• Shell (bats): Timeout with no files → exit 1
• Shell (bats): No fswatch available → exit 2
• Shell (bats): Pre-existing results detected and counted
• Shell (bats): Partial completion → reports found, exits 1

Source: internal/specs/execute-tasks-hardening-SPEC.md Section 5.4
